sections.heading: What Geetanjali Is
content: Geetanjali is a free, open-source web application that provides AI-generated guidance based on the Bhagavad Gita. It is offered as-is, without warranty of any kind.

Geetanjali is not a substitute for professional counseling, therapy, legal advice, medical advice, or any other form of professional guidance. The responses are generated by AI and may contain inaccuracies.

sections.heading: Using the Service
content: You may use Geetanjali for personal, non-commercial reflection and guidance. You do not need an account to use the core service.
You agree not to submit content that is abusive, harmful, illegal, or intended to exploit the service. Geetanjali includes content moderation and rate limiting to prevent misuse.
We reserve the right to restrict access if the service is being used in ways that harm other users or the infrastructure.

sections.heading: Your Content
content: The questions and dilemmas you submit remain yours. We do not claim ownership of your content.
For guest users, your content is stored only in your browser. For registered users, your content is stored on our server and can be exported or deleted at any time.

By submitting a question, you consent to it being processed by third-party AI providers (currently Anthropic and Google) for the purpose of generating guidance. See our **Privacy Policy** for details.

sections.heading: AI-Generated Content
content: All guidance provided by Geetanjali is generated by AI language models using verses from the Bhagavad Gita as context. Responses may be inaccurate, incomplete, or inappropriate for your specific situation.
Verse citations are provided so you can read the original source material and form your own understanding. The AI's interpretation is one perspective among many.
You are responsible for your own decisions. Geetanjali provides perspectives for reflection, not directives for action.

sections.heading: Availability
content: Geetanjali is provided free of charge. We make reasonable efforts to keep the service available but do not guarantee uptime or uninterrupted access.
We may modify, suspend, or discontinue the service at any time. We will make reasonable efforts to provide notice of significant changes.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
content: The Bhagavad Gita verses and translations are sourced from public domain texts. Full attribution is available on our GitHub repository.
The Geetanjali application code is open source and available under the license specified in the repository.
Audio recitations are original recordings. You may listen to them through the app but may not redistribute them without permission.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
content: Geetanjali is provided as-is. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we disclaim all warranties and shall not be liable for any damages arising from your use of the service.
This includes, without limitation, any decisions made based on AI-generated guidance, any data loss, or any service interruptions.

sections.heading: Changes to These Terms
content: We may update these terms as the service evolves. Significant changes will be noted on this page with an updated date. Continued use of Geetanjali after changes constitutes acceptance.
